news.title: EOW arrests director in ₹100-crore real estate ponzi scam in Uttar Pradesh; five associates still at large
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Police's Economic Offences Wing arrested Mahipal Singh, a director of Karmabhoomi Real Estate Company Limited, in connection with a ₹100 crore fraud. The company allegedly duped hundreds of investors by promising high returns and plot allotments but failed to deliver. Five other directors remain absconding.

news.title: Villages spared as rural registrations see modest hike in Telangana
news.para: Telangana government has significantly increased land rates within Hyderabad's Outer Ring Road, with some areas seeing a 100% hike. Property registration charges in many rural areas and district headquarters have seen moderate increases of 10-20%, with some villages experiencing no revision at all, prioritizing farmers' interests.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh earmarks ₹350 crore for high-rise fire tenders in 11 cities
news.para: Uttar Pradesh's housing department has allocated Rs 350 crore for advanced fire tenders with hydraulic platforms to combat fires in high-rise buildings. This initiative aims to bolster firefighting capabilities in 11 rapidly urbanizing cities, with Ghaziabad set to receive the most advanced equipment capable of reaching 102 meters.

news.title: Delhi HC impleads JK Paper as defendant in the legal dispute over Prithviraj Road bungalow
news.para: JK Paper Ltd is now a defendant in a Delhi High Court case. The dispute concerns a bungalow on Prithviraj Road. Punit Beriwala claims he made an advance payment for the property in 2004. JK Paper acquired the bungalow in December 2021 for Rs 250 crore. Beriwala's counsel informed JK Paper about his client's claim.

news.title: Truhome Finance, Veegaland Developers get Sebi nod for IPO
news.para: Truhome Finance's proposed IPO comprises a fresh issue of equity shares worth Rs 1,500 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) aggregating to Rs 1,500 crore by promoter Mango Crest Investment. Veegaland Developers, a part of the V-Guard Group, plans to raise Rs 250 crore through an entirely fresh issue of shares. There is no OFS component in the proposed issue.

news.title: Delhi government to invoke DMA Act, empower DMs to act against unauthorised construction
news.para: Delhi government will recover losses from corrupt officials and invoke the Disaster Management Act 2005 following a fatal hotel fire. Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced that negligent officials will face up to two years imprisonment and fines, with recovery of losses from their salaries, pensions, and properties.

news.title: Karnataka HC upholds land acquisition for Bengaluru’s Banashankari VI Stage project
news.para: The Karnataka High Court has upheld the land acquisition for Bengaluru's Banashankari VI Stage project, reversing a single bench's decision. The division bench ruled that once land is acquired free of encumbrances, it cannot be quashed, imposing a Rs 50,000 cost on landowners for abusing the court process.

news.title: Karnataka records surge in female ownership of homes and land
news.para: Female home and land ownership in Karnataka has nearly doubled in five years, with 29.5% of households now having at least one female owner. This surge reflects changing attitudes towards financial security, independence, and fairer wealth distribution within families, with rural areas showing higher joint ownership.

news.title: Delhi civic body demolishes 82 properties, seals 43 in June 2026
news.para: Following recent fatal incidents, the Municipal Corporation of Delhi has intensified its crackdown on unauthorized construction. Since June 1, the MCD has demolished 82 properties and sealed 43, including several bed and breakfast guesthouses found violating building bye-laws. This action is being taken under the direction of the lieutenant governor and chief minister to prevent future tragedies.

news.title: Registration charges surge in Hyderabad's major growth corridors
news.para: Hyderabad's western corridor faces a significant surge in property registration charges, with rates in prime areas like Raidurg Panmaktha, Madhapur, and Khajaguda jumping to Rs 24.22 crore per acre.  The state government's revised land rates, effective Friday, have also seen substantial increases in IT corridor localities, while core city neighborhoods experienced more modest hikes or remained unchanged.

news.title: Ghaziabad asks all RWAs to appoint two firefighters in shifts
news.para: Ghaziabad administration will demolish illegal constructions and encroachments within housing societies, identifying them as fire hazards. Resident welfare associations must appoint two trained firefighters per society, working in 12-hour shifts, to enhance emergency response and evacuation efforts following recent fire incidents.

news.title: STT Global acquires 3.5-acre land parcel in Navi Mumbai for ₹282 crore
news.para: STT Global Data Centres India has bought a 3.48-acre industrial property in Navi Mumbai's Mahape for Rs 282 crore. This strategic acquisition supports the growing demand for digital infrastructure. The site in the TTC Industrial Area offers access to power and connectivity.

news.title: MHADA starts handover of 537 rehabilitation flats in Naigaon BDD Chawl project
news.para: MHADA has started issuing allotment letters and possession of 537 rehabilitation flats in the Naigaon BDD Chawl redevelopment project. On the first day, 15 eligible residents received allotment letters and possession of their new homes after completing the required documentation.

news.title: Elevated elegance shaped by the Earth and Sea at Godrej Skyshore, Versova
news.para: Godrej Skyshore in Versova, Andheri West, has been recognized at The Economic Times Real Estate Awards 2026 for its high-end residential project. This development features two towers with 24 floors, a sky bridge, and extensive amenities, offering sea views and a sustainable living experience. Its strong market response and strategic location highlight a new era of urban coastal living.

news.title: Chandigarh civic body tweaks tender norms to attract bidders for C&D plant
news.para: Chandigarh's municipal corporation is trying again to find a partner for its Construction and Demolition waste plant after three failed attempts.  Revised tender conditions now offer a revenue-sharing model, hoping to attract bidders. The plant will be operated for five years, with the successful firm handling waste processing and selling recycled materials.

news.title: Nearly 69% of latest Sec 39A proposals in Bardez
news.para: The TCP department processed proposals for 88,249 sqm of land under Section 39A, with the state government approving 34,527 sqm and recommending another 53,722 sqm for public scrutiny. Bardez dominates land conversion, accounting for nearly 69% of the total area, with significant proposals in Tivim and Nadora. These approvals are subject to departmental clearances and pending court litigations.

news.title: Nearly 37% of Kalyan-Dombivli smart city equipment defunct
news.para: A significant portion of Kalyan-Dombivli's Rs 120-crore Smart City project infrastructure, including nearly 37% of devices, is non-functional.  Police officials are concerned as 392 out of 1,065 installed devices, such as CCTV and ANPR cameras, have stopped working, impacting public safety and crime detection efforts.

news.title: High-level meet paves way for regularisation of Ulhasnagar properties
news.para: Maharashtra government is set to regularize unauthorized properties in Ulhasnagar built before 2011, a move benefiting lakhs. Occupants will now pay only 5% of the Ready Reckoner value for ownership, a significant reduction. A special task force will expedite the process, issuing Property Cards and resolving decades-old land disputes.

news.title: Pay for traffic jams you create: Ahmedabad civic body's message to developers
news.para: Ahmedabad is introducing a new traffic policy. Developers will now pay for the congestion their projects cause. A traffic impact assessment will be mandatory for large commercial developments. Building ratings will assess environmental and community impact. Malls and offices must share private parking. Penalties for violations are significant. This aims to improve traffic flow and road safety across the city.

news.title: 91Springboard bets big on the ‘hotelisation’ of office spaces
news.para: 91Springboard is evolving from a coworking pioneer into a full-stack workspace platform, emphasizing hospitality-led experiences, technology, and service precision. The company is expanding its presence in key commercial hubs, catering to growing enterprise demand for managed, customized office solutions that blend flexibility with reliability.
